page: Amazon Web Services c7g.4xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton processor family, utilizing the arm64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz with an ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 microarchitecture. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with the PassMark memory score placing in the top 10% tier. Large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks, both read and write, rank in the top 25% tier, achieving 186.18 GB/sec and 93.97 GB/sec respectively. CPU-centric benchmarks, including stress-ng, Geekbench, and PassMark CPU scores, generally fall within the middle 50% average. However, the server exhibits weaker performance in specific areas, with the PassMark CPU encryption test and medium-model LLM prompt processing falling into the bottom 25% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture offers a high density of dedicated physical cores relative to memory, making it a viable option for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. The server is suited for workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression. It is less suited for cryptographic-heavy tasks or large-scale LLM inference due to the lack of hardware accelerators and lower encryption benchmark scores.
description: Amazon Web Services c7g.4xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, with no local storage or GPU hardware. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier memory performance, with its PassMark memory score in the top 10% and large-block memory bandwidth in the top 25%. Conversely, CPU encryption and medium-model LLM prompt processing fall into the bottom 25% tier. The instance is designed for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and database operations where dedicated physical cores and high memory bandwidth are prioritized over local storage or specialized cryptographic accelerators.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.4xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server with 16 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, suited for compilation and web serving.

tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized server featuring sixteen dedicated physical cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for compute-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated physical ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 cores running at 2.6 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory with top-tier memory bandwidth performance
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included
Weaker performance in cryptographic and medium-model LLM prompt processing benchmarks
Suited for software compilation, static web serving, and database operations
